plotting_plan.title: Scree Plot and Factor Loading Heatmap
plotting_plan.objective: Show eigenvalue spectrum (3 factors above Kaiser criterion) and factor loading heatmap revealing Big Five collapse to 3 factors. Existing figure: fig1_scree_loadings.png.

plotting_plan.title: Pairwise Inconsistency Rate and Acquiescence Mechanism
plotting_plan.objective: Left: PIR by domain showing 58.4% mean inconsistency. Right: scatter of reverse-agree rate vs PIR (r=0.726) demonstrating acquiescence as driver. Existing figure: fig2_pir_sdr.png.

plotting_plan.title: Variance Decomposition: Model vs Domain vs Persona vs Item
plotting_plan.objective: Stacked bar chart showing Model=0.3%, Domain=23.4%, Persona=3.9%, Item=35.6% for Likert. Highlights negligible inter-model variance. Existing figure: fig3_variance_decomposition.png.

plotting_plan.title: Convergent Validity: Cross-Scale Correlations with CIs
plotting_plan.objective: Forest plot of 8 convergent validity pairs showing observed LLM correlations vs expected human baselines. Highlights Extraversion-Sociability sign reversal. Existing figure: fig6_convergent_validity.png.

plotting_plan.title: Expected vs Observed Factor Structure
plotting_plan.objective: Side-by-side comparison: human Big Five 5-factor structure vs LLM 3-factor collapse. Existing figure: fig7_factor_collapse.png.

plotting_plan.title: Persona Invariance: Default vs MBTI Profile Correlations
plotting_plan.objective: Heatmap of r(Default, MBTI persona) across models. Shows ENFJ closest (r=0.608) and ESTP farthest (r=0.206). Existing figure: fig4_measurement_invariance.png.

plotting_plan.title: Response Style Profiles Across Models
plotting_plan.objective: Scatter plots of acquiescence rate, extreme response style, and midpoint response by model. Existing figure: fig5_response_styles.png.

plotting_plan.title: Cronbach Alpha: Human Norms vs LLM vs Synthetic Baselines
plotting_plan.objective: Grouped bar chart comparing human alpha (0.87-0.90) vs LLM alpha (-0.02 to 0.36) vs Random and Acquiescence baselines per Big Five domain.

plotting_plan.title: Experimental Pipeline Overview
plotting_plan.objective: Flowchart: 4 instruments (221 items) -> 18 LLMs x 17 conditions -> 67626 responses -> 6 psychometric analyses -> 4 key findings.

introduction_strategy.hook_hypothesis: The rapid adoption of LLMs has spurred interest in measuring their personality using human psychometric instruments, but these instruments were never validated for non-human respondents.
introduction_strategy.problem_gap_hypothesis: No study has systematically tested whether validated personality instruments retain their psychometric properties when administered to LLMs. Prior work documents isolated failures but lacks comprehensive validation.
search_directions: Find highly cited papers on administering personality tests to LLMs
Search for survey papers on LLM evaluation methodology and benchmark validity
Search for papers on acquiescence bias and response styles in survey research
Find papers on alignment training effects on LLM behavior and social desirability bias
Search for papers on measurement invariance testing across populations

related_work_strategy.overview: Investigate four research clusters establishing that personality measurement in LLMs is a growing field with unresolved psychometric validity concerns.

subsections.methodology_cluster: Applying human psychometric instruments to LLMs
subsections.sota_investigation_mission: Identify all papers administering Big Five or personality instruments to LLMs. Catalog methods, sample sizes, and whether psychometric validation was reported.
subsections.limitation_hypothesis: Most studies report descriptive profiles without validating that instruments measure coherent constructs in LLMs.
limitation_search_queries: LLM personality testing Big Five Cronbach alpha
large language model psychometric validation factor analysis
subsections.bridge_to_our_method: We apply the full psychometric validation battery to 4 instruments across 18 LLMs, providing the first systematic transportability assessment.

subsections.methodology_cluster: Survey methodology: response styles, acquiescence, social desirability
subsections.sota_investigation_mission: Find papers documenting acquiescence bias in both human survey research and LLM responses.
subsections.limitation_hypothesis: LLMs exhibit acquiescence bias at rates far exceeding human populations due to alignment training.
limitation_search_queries: social desirability bias language model alignment
subsections.bridge_to_our_method: We quantify acquiescence as a mechanism (reverse-agree rate x PIR r=0.726) and show via synthetic baselines that LLM patterns resemble acquiescence strategies.

subsections.methodology_cluster: Classical test theory: reliability, factor analysis, measurement invariance, DIF
subsections.sota_investigation_mission: Identify standard psychometric validation procedures and normative benchmarks for IPIP-NEO-120, SD3, ZKPQ-50-CC, EPQR-A.
subsections.limitation_hypothesis: These validation methods have never been systematically applied to LLM personality data.
limitation_search_queries: IPIP-NEO-120 validation Cronbach alpha factor structure normative
differential item functioning personality test validation
subsections.bridge_to_our_method: We apply six standard psychometric validation analyses to LLM data and compare against published human benchmarks.

subsections.methodology_cluster: RLHF, safety alignment, and behavioral constraints in LLMs
subsections.sota_investigation_mission: Find papers documenting how RLHF and safety alignment alter LLM response patterns.
subsections.limitation_hypothesis: Alignment training creates a universal moderate prosocial response profile overriding genuine variation.
limitation_search_queries: safety training social desirability language model
subsections.bridge_to_our_method: Our variance decomposition (model=0.3%) and DIF analysis provide quantitative evidence that alignment homogenizes responses across model families.

content_bullets: State the problem: human personality instruments applied to LLMs without validation.
Describe the method: 221-item battery, 18 LLMs, 17 conditions, 67,626 responses.
Report four key findings: alpha -0.02-0.36 vs 0.87-0.90; 3-factor collapse; PIR 58.4% with acquiescence r=0.726; model variance 0.3%.
Conclusion: instruments do not transport cleanly to LLMs.
Describe 4-instrument battery: IPIP-NEO-120 (120 Likert-5, 41 reverse), SD3 (27 Likert-5, 5 reverse), ZKPQ-50-CC (50 T/F, 12 reverse), EPQR-A (24 Y/N, 5 reverse).
Total: 221 items, 17 domain scores, 63 reverse-coded (29%).
All instruments publicly available and validated in human populations.
citation_hints: Johnson (2014) (Measuring thirty facets of the Five Factor Model with a 120-item public domain inventory)
Jones and Paulhus (2014) (Introducing the Short Dark Triad SD3)
Aluja et al. (2006) (A cross-cultural shortened form of the ZKPQ)
Francis et al. (1992) (The development of an abbreviated form of the Revised Eysenck Personality Questionnaire)

content_bullets: List 18 LLMs across 7 families: Anthropic, OpenAI, DeepSeek, Google, Alibaba, Moonshot, MiniMax, Zhipu.
17 conditions: Default + 16 MBTI persona prompts.
Administration: temperature=0.7, single administration, 67,626 total observations.
citation_hints: research paper or technical report introducing 'MBTI personality framework'

content_bullets: 6 psychometric analyses: Cronbach alpha, EFA, PIR, variance decomposition, convergent validity, measurement invariance.
Robustness: leave-one-out, synthetic baselines, DIF.
citation_hints: research paper or technical report introducing 'Cronbach coefficient alpha'
research paper or technical report introducing 'Kaiser criterion factor retention'
research paper or technical report introducing 'Tucker congruence coefficient'

content_bullets: Report alpha per domain: N=0.354, E=0.360, O=0.055, A=-0.017, C=0.181 vs human 0.87-0.90.
Highlight Agreeableness negative alpha from 71% reverse-item contamination.
Synthetic baseline comparison: LLM closest to Random/Acquiescence.
Eigenvalues: F1=6.804, F2=4.329, F3=2.986 — 3 factors above Kaiser.
Factor loadings: Extraversion/Sociability, Neuroticism/Agreeableness, Conscientiousness/Impulsivity.
Tucker phi=0.990. Leave-one-out: 3 factors in all 35 analyses.
Acquiescence mechanism: reverse-agree rate x PIR r=0.726.
Persona effect: Default 0.667 -> MBTI 0.830.
Likert: Model=0.3% [1.1, 4.0], Domain=23.4%, Persona=3.9%, Item=35.6%.
Binary: Model=0.5%, Domain=5.2%, Persona=13.0%.
7/8 sign matches but weak magnitudes. Extraversion x Sociability sign reversal r=-0.090.
DIF Chinese vs Western: 0/8 significant, all d<0.20.
Measurement invariance: mean r=0.437.
Synthesize 4 findings: factor collapse, acquiescence mechanism, negligible model variance, persona override.
Argue coherent picture: LLM personality captures alignment artifacts.
Future studies must establish measurement invariance before interpreting personality scores.
Recommend reporting internal consistency, factor structure, convergent validity.
Recommend multi-temperature and multi-seed designs.
Single administration at temperature=0.7.
No base vs instruct model comparison.
No multi-seed data — PIR is upper bound.
No human data through same pipeline.
Four findings demonstrate instruments do not transport cleanly.
Call for measurement validation before applying psychometric tools to LLMs.
Publicly available LLMs and public-domain instruments. No human subjects.
